questions.question: Starting from the blue ball hopper with the solved board configuration, describe the complete path of the first blue ball.
questions.answer: The first blue ball drops from (2,-1), rolls onto the crossover at (5,1), then through crossovers at (5,2), (5,3), (5,4), (5,5), then ramps: ramp_right at (4,6), ramp_right at (5,7), ramp_right at (6,8), ramp_right at (7,9), ramp_right at (8,10), reaching the right lever.

questions.question: What is the output pattern after running the machine?
questions.answer: The pattern is blue, red, blue, red, blue, red... alternating.

questions.question: How does the crossover enable the alternating pattern?
questions.answer: When a blue ball crosses over to the right side, it triggers the right lever to release a red ball. That red ball crosses back over to the left side, triggering the left lever to release another blue ball, creating the alternating pattern.
